plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and meet mandate limits.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure.
Increase QQQI to 25% for income and stability.
Buy XLF (5%) and PANW (5%) to diversify into high-scoring, quality names.
Maintain a significant cash buffer by reducing overall exposure.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets capital preservation and aims for ~1.5% monthly returns. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration, leading to lower expected drawdowns. However, potential upside is also limited.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits and reduce gross leverage.
Sell SOXL as it is below its stop and to reduce overall leverage.
Trim SPAL to 2% to reduce leveraged ETF exposure.
Increase CRWD to 18% to lean into a strong uptrending name.
Buy XLF (5%) and PANW (5%) for diversification and strong scanner setups.
Maintain a 10% cash buffer for flexibility.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets ~2.5% monthly returns with a risk-first growth approach. It reduces overall leverage and concentration while still participating in quality growth. Expect moderate drawdowns, but with better upside potential than conservative.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to comply with concentration limits.
Sell SOXL as it is below its stop and reallocate capital.
Trim SPAL to 3% to keep some leveraged exposure while managing risk.
Increase CRWD to 20% to lean into a strong uptrend and positive P&L.
Buy PANW (7%) as it has the highest R-ratio among scanner setups.
Watch NVDU (5%) for potential entry to add high-score chip exposure.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets ~4% monthly returns by leaning into leverage and high-scoring setups. It significantly increases exposure to high-beta assets. Be aware of substantially higher drawdown potential and the daily decay of leveraged ETFs; strict stop discipline is critical.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits and free up cash.
Increase QQQI to 30% to maximize income generation.
Reduce QQQ to 10% and CRWD to 5% to lower growth and high-beta exposure.
Trim SMH to 5% to reduce semiconductor concentration.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF risk.
Prioritize a large cash buffer for stability.
income.read: This income plan targets ~1% monthly distributions by rotating into income-focused ETFs. It significantly cuts leverage and high-beta single names for lower drawdown. While aiming for steady income, distributions are variable, and NAV can still decline in market downturns.
